services.name: pub.synchronization.latch:closeLatch
services.description: WmPublic. Closes the latch for a resource.

input_parameters.name: appId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the target resource for which you want to close a latch. Typically, the appId is the name of the adapter or the resource.

input_parameters.name: canonicalKey
input_parameters.description: String The canonical key. A unique identifier for the canonical document used in the synchronization.

input_parameters.name: objectId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the object or process being synchronized. Typically, the objectId field is set to the name of the business process for which you are performing synchronization, such as "order" or "customer."

services.name: pub.synchronization.latch:isLatchClosed
services.description: WmPublic. Checks a resource's latch status.

input_parameters.name: appId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the resource for which you want to check the latch status. Typically, the appId is the name of the adapter or the resource.

input_parameters.name: canonicalKey
input_parameters.description: String The canonical key. A unique identifier for the canonical document used in the synchronization.

input_parameters.name: objectId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the object or process being synchronized. Typically, the objectId field is set to the name of the business process for which you are performing synchronization, such as "order" or "customer."

output_parameters.name: isLatchClosed
output_parameters.description: String The status of the latch. A value of: true indicates that the latch is closed. The resource has been updated. false indicates that the latch is open. The resource has not been updated.

usage_notes: Use the latch status to determine whether or not to update the resource.
For more information about using the pub.synchronization.latch services to prevent echo suppression, see the Publish-Subscribe Developer’s Guide .

services.name: pub.synchronization.latch:openLatch
services.description: WmPublic. Opens the latch for a resource.

input_parameters.name: appId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the resources for which you want to open the latch. Typically, the appId is the name of the adapter or the resource.

input_parameters.name: canonicalKey
input_parameters.description: String The canonical key. A unique identifier for the canonical document used in the synchronization.

input_parameters.name: objectId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the object or process being synchronized. Typically, the objectId field is set to the name of the business process for which you are performing synchronization, such as "order" or "customer."

services.name: pub.synchronization.xref:createXReference
services.description: WmPublic. Creates a cross-reference between a canonical key and a native ID.

input_parameters.name: appId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the resource (application) for which you want to create a cross-reference to a canonical key.

input_parameters.name: nativeId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the resource record for which you want to create a cross-reference to a canonical key.

input_parameters.name: canonicalKey
input_parameters.description: String Optional. A canonical key. If a canonical key is not provided as input, createXReference creates the canonical key and the cross-reference.

input_parameters.name: objectId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the object or process being synchronized. Typically, the objectId field is set to the name of the business process for which you are performing synchronization, such as "order" or "customer."

output_parameters.name: canonicalKey
output_parameters.description: String The canonical key. This key correlates native IDs of records from different resources. This will be a new, unique key if canonicalKey was not provided as an input parameter. If canonicalKey was provided as input, this output parameter returns the same value.

usage_notes: The canonical document is the standard format that a document assumes while it travels through webMethods components. A source resource will convert or map data from its proprietary data format into the canonical format before publishing the document. A target resource (a subscriber to the canonical document) will map the canonical document to the target resource's proprietary data format before processing the document. The canonical document acts as the intermediary data format between resources.

On the source side of the synchronization, use the createXReference service to create the canonical key for the canonical document and establish a cross-reference between the record in the source application and the canonical document. Before publishing the canonical document, link the generated canonicalKey to the canonical document.

On the target side of synchronization, use the pub.synchronization.xref:insertXReference service to insert the cross-reference between a canonical key and the native ID for the record in the target resource.
For more information about using the createXReference service to create synchronizations, see the section Built-In Services for Key Cross-Referencing in the Publish-Subscribe Developer’s Guide .

services.name: pub.synchronization.xref:deleteByObjectId
services.description: WmPublic. Removes all cross-reference records associated with a particular process or synchronization.

input_parameters.name: objectId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the object or process for which you want to delete all cross-reference records. Typically, the objectId field is set to the name of the business process for which you are performing synchronization, such as "order" or "customer."

usage_notes: You can use this service to purge unwanted cross-reference records from the cross-reference table. For example, if you wanted to delete all cross-reference records for the purchaseOrder synchronization, specify "purchaseOrder" as the objectId.

services.name: pub.synchronization.xref:deleteXReference
services.description: WmPublic. Deletes a cross-reference record from the cross-reference table.

input_parameters.name: appId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the resource (application) for which you want to delete a cross-reference record.

input_parameters.name: canonicalKey
input_parameters.description: String The canonical key. A unique identifier for the canonical document for which you want to delete a cross-reference.

input_parameters.name: objectId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the object or process for which you want to delete a cross-reference. Typically, the objectId field is set to the name of the business process for which you are performing synchronization, such as "order" or "customer."

services.name: pub.synchronization.xref:getCanonicalKey
services.description: WmPublic. Retrieves the canonical key for a specified native ID.

input_parameters.name: appId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the resource (application) that contains the native ID for which you want to retrieve a canonical key.

input_parameters.name: nativeId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the resource record for which you want to obtain the canonical key.

input_parameters.name: objectId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the object or process being synchronized. Typically, the objectId field is set to the name of the business process for which you are performing synchronization, such as "order" or "customer."

output_parameters.name: canonicalKey
output_parameters.description: String The canonical key for the provided native ID. If the requested key cannot be found or does not exist in the cross-reference table, an empty string is returned.

usage_notes: You can use this service to determine whether you need to insert or update a record in the resource.
For more information about using the getCanonicalKey service in synchronizations, see the Publish-Subscribe Developer’s Guide .

services.name: pub.synchronization.xref:getNativeId
services.description: WmPublic. Retrieves the native ID of a resource record associated with a canonical key.

input_parameters.name: appId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the resource from which you want to retrieve the native ID associated with the provided canonical key.

input_parameters.name: canonicalKey
input_parameters.description: String The canonical key for which you want to obtain the corresponding native ID.

input_parameters.name: objectId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the object or process being synchronized. Typically, the objectId field is set to the name of the business process for which you are performing synchronization, such as "order" or "customer."

output_parameters.name: nativeId
output_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the resource record associated with the provided canonical key. If the requested nativeId cannot be found in the cross-reference table, an empty string is returned.

usage_notes: You can use the getNativeId service on the target side of a synchronization to determine if the record in the target resource needs to be inserted or just updated.
After you insert or update the record in the resource, make sure to use pub.synchronization.latch:closeLatch to close the latch for the record to prevent circular updates (echoes).
For more information about using the getNativeId service in synchronizations, see the Publish-Subscribe Developer’s Guide .

services.name: pub.synchronization.xref:insertXReference
services.description: WmPublic. Inserts a cross-reference between a native ID and a canonical key.

input_parameters.name: appId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the resource for which you want to establish a cross-reference between a native ID and a canonical key.

input_parameters.name: nativeId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the resource record with which you want to establish a cross-reference to canonicalKey.

input_parameters.name: canonicalKey
input_parameters.description: String The canonical key with which you want to establish a cross-reference to nativeId.

input_parameters.name: objectId
input_parameters.description: String A unique identifier for the object or process being synchronized. Typically, the objectId field is set to the name of the business process for which you are performing synchronization, such as "order" or "customer."

usage_notes: Use this service on the target side of a synchronization to create a cross-reference between the new record in the target resource and the canonical document.
Most resources generate a unique ID for a new record. Invoke the insertXReference service after you add the new record in the resource.
After you insert the cross-reference between the new native ID and the canonical key, use pub.synchronization.latch:closeLatch to close the latch for the record to prevent circular updates (echoes).
For more information about using the insertXReference service in synchronizations, see the Publish-Subscribe Developer’s Guide .
